metadata.paper_title: Software architectural analysis: an experience report
metadata.venue: CASCON '95: Proceedings of the 1995 conference of the Centre for Advanced Studies on Collaborative research

system_context.system_description: A commercial revision control system that aids in the management and organization of files in a computer-based development project, tracking changes, and allowing multiple users to work on the same project.
system_context.business_goals: To improve non-functional qualities such as portability, modifiability and extendibility.

evaluation_setup.evaluation_goal: To evaluate the system's non-functional qualities, specifically modifiability and portability, and determine directions for product evolution.

architectures.name: WRCS Architecture
architectures.description: Reverse engineered architecture consisting of executables and dynamic link libraries (DLLs) interacting via calls, invocations, and DDE communication.

key_components.responsibilities: Central executable component interacting with UI and other DLLs
Provides a gate to WRCS' functionality; supports menu extensors

assumptions: Software architecture is supposed to concern itself with a level of abstraction above code

scenarios.scenario_text: Understand other products binary file representation. Give meaning to binary files generated by other products.
Configure the product's toolbar. Change the icons and actions associated with a button in the toolbar.
Port to another operating system. Go from Microsoft Windows to for example IBM OS/2.
Make minor modifications to the user interface. Add a menu item, change the look and feel of a dialog box.
Port to a different network management system within the same operating system. Change from Novell's NetWare to, for example, Microsoft Lantastic.
Integrate with a new development environment. Attach for example to Symantec C++.

scenario_classification.rationale: Requires changes in diffb.dll and potentially visdiff.exe.
Requires modification of all components calling Windows 3.1 API (main.exe, visdiff.exe, ctrls.dll, OWL) and changes to wrcs.dll, menu extensors, and hook.dll.
Requires modification of main.exe, visdiff.exe, hook.dll, and possibly ctrls.dll.
Not supported within current functionality, though hook.dll supports the addition.

scenario_evaluations.change_description: Changes to binary diff logic and visual display
Modification of API calls, networking environment logic, invocation mechanisms, and menu extensors.
issues_identified: Large number of changes indicates no architectural support for portability.
Development tied too closely to WIN31 API without adequate abstraction.
Fairly straightforward due to hook.dll providing a gate.

scenario_interactions.modularity_implication: Component with most scenario interaction (7 changes); poor isolation of functionality.
High scenario interaction indicating potential trouble spots and poor separation of concerns.

architectural_strengths: Extendibility is supported indirectly via hook.dll.
architectural_weaknesses: Modifiability (User Interface) not supported due to lack of abstraction layer.
Modifiability (Archive Format) not supported; functionality scattered.

architecture_comparison.rationale: Single architecture evaluation; product found to have severe limitations requiring major redesign.

recommendations: Use techniques for separating concerns in interface-dominated applications.

missing_information: Full text of all 15 scenarios (only 6 presented in paper).
inference_notes: Inferred 'Component-Based' style from the description of DLLs and EXEs.
Inferred 'Evaluation' roles based on the authors conducting the study.
Grouped interaction analysis based on the 'fish-eye' visualization description in the text.
